_comment: Single source of truth for the benchmark-regression gate (PERF-08) and the
committed baseline snapshot (PERF-09). Consumed by tools/perf/gate.py and
the .github/workflows/bench-gate.yml workflow.
Only a CURATED, deterministic, low-variance subset of the 10 Criterion
benches is gated. The subset was chosen from measured same-runner
head-vs-head (no-op) deltas: every id below moved < 1.5% across two
back-to-back runs, so a 2% gate does not false-fail on them. Sub-millisecond
and allocation-bound microbenches were EXCLUDED because their scheduling /
allocator jitter exceeds 2% even with same-runner comparison:
- nms_scaling/radius_1, radius_2  (<0.4 ms, +3..10% no-op jitter)
- pyramid .../fresh_buffers/*      (per-iter alloc, +11% no-op jitter)
- radon_response/*_up2             (2x internal upsample, ~8% dispersion)
- orientation_fit/*                (single-corner, ns..us scale)
- chess_response/* (radon-target secondary group; duplicate of
chess_response_kernel and noisy on small frames)
Whole targets excluded as too coarse/variable for a 2% gate: refiners,
upscale, chess_pipeline, radon_pipeline (end-to-end), plus the third-party
pyramid comparison builders (fast_image_resize / resize / image) which are
not our code. The committed snapshot tracks the SAME gated ids.
Threshold is enforced on Criterion's MEDIAN point estimate (the number
critcmp reports). True p95 would require parsing target/criterion/*/new/
sample.json; median is the pragmatic, standard mechanism. Override the
budget per-run with REGRESSION_THRESHOLD_PCT.
